template.p: This setup is for companies providing solar equipment and its installation service. Usually, residential customers place orders from 1 kW to 10 kW while others up to 100 kW. Solar panels and other equipment are installed at customer site based on kW capacity.
CRM App: Capture leads from your website and track them through the sales funnel, managing site visits and technical feasibility checks.
Sales App: Create professional, detailed proposals that include technical specifications for panels, inverters, and labor costs.
Project App: Manage installation projects with clear stages (e.g., "Permitting," "Installation," "Inspection"), tracking tasks and deadlines.
Inventory App: Keep track of high-value stock like solar modules and batteries, ensuring you have the right components for every job.
Website App: Showcase your portfolio of successful installations and attract new eco-conscious clients with a professional online presence.
Sign App: Securely collect digital signatures for contracts, interconnection agreements, and financing documents.

h2.span: Flow 1: Website Form -> CRM -> Sales -> Accounting -> Planning

template.p: Open website Request a quote form

Potential customer fills up the
Request a quote
form and Submit it, in CRM opportunity is created.
CRM Stages

Sales Person is assigned and Opportunity is moved to Qualified stage. Sales Person follows up with customers through Activities like Calls, Meetings, etc. If the customer agrees the quotation, a site survey is planned and Opportunity is moved to the Site Survey stage.
Final Quotation for installation is confirmed and sent to the customer and 100% advance payment is received.
Confirmation of Sales Order creates 1 Planning Shift automatically for Solar system installation.

p.span: Planning for Installation and Delivery of Equipments

template.p: Shift is assigned to employees/workers. Site visit is planned and shift is moved to Planned stage. Assignees takes all the equipments listed in Delivery Order along with them while visiting Client's site for Installation.

Assignees fill up the Worksheet after completion of the Installation. Several images of Solar System installation of Client's site are captured along with the customer's confirmation and signature in Worksheet. The worksheet includes customized fields like Start date, End date, plan, signature, etc which are configured through the Planning Worksheet Templates.

h2.span: Flow 2: Manual Opportunity -> CRM -> Sales -> Accounting -> Planning

template.p: Opportunity is created manually in CRM. From CRM, the flow is same as above
Helpdesk Ticket

- Customer can raise a ticket to Customer Care team for Repair through mail, "Submit a Ticket" form or clicking on Chat bot button in Help page in Website. Support Ticket will be generated in Helpdesk app.
- Helpdesk User will create Repair in draft mode and fills up Customer's details in it like equipment to be repaired, warranty end date, etc.
- Warranty end date is fetched from delivery of the equipment.
Repair

- Inventory user will validate returned equipment in Return and check the warranty end date. If the warranty date is not under the warranty period, an invoice will be raised for the parts replaced and repairing charge.
- Assigned user will confirm the Repair, start the repair, finish the repairing work and inform the Helpdesk user for the same through Chatter functionality.
- Helpdesk user will confirm with the customer whether they will pick up the equipment from repairing site or request to deliver the equipment to their address.
- In case of pick up, ticket will be closed after customer's confirmation of checking equipment at repairing site.
- In case of delivery to customer's address, Helpdesk user will communicate with the customer after delivery and take confirmation from customer about equipment working and closes the Ticket.
